zh.lifeSummary: Catherine Mattson Peterson Rambo於1640年出生在瑞典維亞姆藍德根納申可格。她於1662年與Peter Nilsson Lycon在瑞典維亞姆藍德根納申可格結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1691年11月在美國賓夕法尼亞州貝可斯享年51歲，埋葬在美國賓夕法尼亞州費拉德爾福亞費拉德爾福亞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Catherine Mattson Peterson Rambo于1640年出生在瑞典維亞姆藍德根納申可格。她于1662年与Peter Nilsson Lycon在瑞典維亞姆藍德根納申可格结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1691年11月在美国宾夕法尼亚州貝可斯享年51岁，埋葬在美国宾夕法尼亚州費拉德爾福亞費拉德爾福亞。

nameMeanings.name: Rambo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Swedish: ornamental name from the root of Ramberget, the name of a hill on Hisingen island in Gothenburg, Sweden + bo ‘inhabitant’.
East German: variant of   Rambow  .
In some cases probably also French: altered form of   Rambeau  .

variants.name: Rambow
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Ewald.
East German: habitational name from either of two places near Perleberg, or others in northeastern Germany, called Rambow.

nameMeanings.name: Catherine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Katherine .  This form of the name is also used in France.

variants.name: Catheryn
meanings: Variant spelling of   Katherine .  This form of the name is also used in France.

meanings: Variant of   Katherine .

nameMeanings.name: Mattson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish   Mattsson  .
Americanized form of Danish and Norwegian   Mattsen   and   Madsen  , cognates of 1.
English (mainly Yorkshire and Derbyshire): variant of   Matson  .

variants.name: Mattsson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Anders, Erik, Matts, Nils, Bjorn, Gunvor, Per, Sten, Thor, Thorvald, Torvald.
Swedish: patronymic from the personal name Matts, a short form of Matthias or Mattheus (see Matthew ). Compare Matson and Mattson .

nameMeanings.name: Peterson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and German: patronymic from the personal name Peter . In North America, this surname has absorbed various cognates and their derivatives from other languages, e.g. Norwegian and Danish Pedersen and Pettersen and their Swedish cognates (see 2 below), Polish Piotrowicz , Slovenian Petrič, Petrovčič, and Petrovič (see Petric , Petrovic ).
Americanized form (and a less common Swedish variant) of Swedish Petersson, a cognate of 1 above, and also of its variant Pettersson . Compare 1 above.

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.description: Queen Christina abdicated throne. Converted to Catholicism, later buried in Rome in St. Peter's.

Historical Boundaries: 1682: Pennsylvania, Pennsylvania Colony, British Colonial America 1685: Philadelphia, Pennsylvania Colony, British Colonial America 1776: Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, United States
King Charles II barred Jews from settling in Stockholm.

en.lifeSummary: Catherine Mattson Peterson Rambo was born in 1640, in Gunnarskog, Värmland, Sweden. She married Peter Nilsson Lycon in 1662, in Gunnarskog, Värmland, Sweden. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died in November 1691, in Berks, Pennsylvania, United States, at the age of 51, and was buried in Philadelphia, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Catherine Mattson Peterson Rambo est née en 1640, à Gunnarskog, Värmland, Suède. Elle a épousé Peter Nilsson Lycon en 1662, à Gunnarskog, Värmland, Suède. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en novembre 1691, à Berks, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterrée à Philadelphia, Philadelphia, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Catherine Mattson Peterson Rambo wurde 1640 in Gunnarskog, Värmland, Schweden geboren. Sie hat 1662 in Gunnarskog, Värmland, Schweden Peter Nilsson Lycon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb im November 1691 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Berks, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Philadelphia, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Catherine Mattson Peterson Ramboは1640年にスウェーデンvaルムランドグナルスコグで生まれた。1662年にスウェーデンvaルムランドグナルスコグでPeter Nilsson Lyconと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1691年11月にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州バークスで51歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州フィラデルフィアフィラデルフィアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Catherine Mattson Peterson Rambo(은)는 1640년 스웨덴 브a랜드 건아르스코그에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1662년 스웨덴 브a랜드 건아르스코그에서 Peter Nilsson Lycon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1691년 11월 미국 펜실베이니아 주 버크스에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 펜실베이니아 주 필라들필아 필라들필아에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Catherine Mattson Peterson Rambo родилась в 1640 г. в местности гуннарског, вaрмланд, Швеция. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Peter Nilsson Lycon в 1662 г. в местности гуннарског, вaрмланд, Швеция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла ноябрь 1691 г. в местности биркс, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности филаделфя, филаделфя, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты.
